page: Microsoft Azure E2s_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel Xeon 8370C core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage and does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average category. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are limited. Multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests both rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting the constraints of its single physical core. Memory bandwidth for both read and write operations on large blocks is also in the bottom 10%, alongside weak static web serving throughput and poor database operations performance.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the E2s_v5 is positioned as a memory-optimized instance. It is suitable for workloads that require a larger memory footprint relative to compute power but do not demand high multi-threaded processing or high memory bandwidth. Examples include small-scale memory-cached databases, light text processing, and basic single-threaded applications. From a cost-efficiency perspective, it provides a low-cost entry point to dedicated memory resources, though its compute and memory bandwidth limitations represent a significant tradeoff for intensive multi-threaded workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E2s_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ESv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical Intel Xeon 8370C core running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU and text processing capabilities, but multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the bottom 10% due to the single physical core limitation. Consequently, this instance is best suited for memory-sensitive, single-threaded workloads, small-scale caching, or light text processing where high memory capacity is required without intensive parallel compute demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2s_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, optimized for memory-sensitive, single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2s_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM, designed for memory-optimized, light compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for single-threaded applications and light caching workloads.
